questions.question: In a right triangle, the side opposite a 35° angle is 12 cm. What is the length of the hypotenuse? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use sin(35°) = opposite/hypotenuse. So hypotenuse = 12/sin(35°) ≈ 12/0.574 ≈ 20.9 cm.

questions.question: What is sin(150°) in simplest form?
questions.explanation: 150° = 180° − 30°. By the reference angle, sin(150°) = sin(30°) = 1/2.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, making a 62° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 8 meters long, how high up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 62° angle. Use sin(62°) = height/8, so height = 8·sin(62°) ≈ 8(0.883) ≈ 7.1 meters.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) − cos²(θ)
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean identity sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. Subtracting cos²(θ) from 1 leaves sin²(θ).

questions.question: An observer stands 50 meters from the base of a building. The angle of elevation to the top is 48°. What is the height of the building? Round to the nearest meter.
questions.explanation: Use tan(48°) = height/50, so height = 50·tan(48°) ≈ 50(1.111) ≈ 56 meters.

questions.question: What is the value of tan(45°)?
questions.explanation: At 45°, the opposite and adjacent sides of a 45-45-90 triangle are equal, so tan(45°) = 1.
